Example_Clause: This Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one-year periods unless written notice is provided ninety days prior to expiration.
The contract will renew automatically unless cancelled in writing at least 120 days before the end of the term.
This is an evergreen agreement that continues indefinitely until terminated by either party with 180 days written notice.
Either party may elect not to renew this Agreement by providing written notice 30 days prior to expiration.
This Agreement may be renewed for one additional term upon mutual written agreement sixty days prior to expiration.
This Agreement shall renew only upon mutual written agreement of both parties prior to the expiration date.
The aggregate liability of Supplier shall not exceed the fees paid in the prior three months.
In no event shall Supplier liability exceed one month of average fees under this Agreement.
The total limitation of liability for either party shall be capped at fifty thousand dollars regardless of claim type.
Supplier liability shall not exceed the total fees paid in the prior twelve months under this Agreement.
Aggregate liability shall not exceed two times the annual contract value for direct damages only.
No limitation of liability shall apply in cases of gross negligence, willful misconduct, or breach of confidentiality obligations.
Supplier shall defend claims at its sole discretion and shall have no obligation to indemnify Customer for third party claims.
Customer shall indemnify and hold harmless Supplier against any and all claims arising from Customer use of services.
Customer shall defend against and bear all costs of any regulatory claims related to services provided.
Each party shall indemnify the other for claims arising from its own negligence or willful misconduct.
Both parties shall maintain adequate insurance and provide mutual indemnification for claims arising from their respective acts or omissions.
Supplier shall indemnify Customer for all claims arising from Supplier negligence, errors, or failure to meet agreed specifications.
All invoices are due and payable net 30 days from invoice date.
Payment is due upon receipt of invoice with no exceptions.
Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month compounded monthly from the due date.
Invoices shall be payable within sixty days of receipt by Customer accounts payable department.
All undisputed invoices shall be payable within seventy-five days of Customer receipt.
All undisputed invoices shall be payable within ninety days of Customer receipt in accordance with Customer standard payment terms.
Supplier reserves the right to adjust pricing annually with no cap on increases based on market conditions.
Pricing shall adjust annually based on the Consumer Price Index without limitation.
Supplier may implement a price adjustment at any time with thirty days written notice.
Annual price adjustments shall not exceed three percent per year based on documented CPI changes.
Pricing increases are limited to a maximum of two and a half percent annually with ninety days advance written notice.
Pricing shall remain fixed for the initial two-year term with any increases capped at two percent annually thereafter subject to mutual written agreement.
Early termination by Customer shall incur a termination fee equal to six months of average monthly fees.
Either party may terminate with one hundred eighty days written notice with no termination for cause provision.
Customer may not terminate this Agreement for convenience during the initial term under any circumstances.
Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon sixty days written notice without penalty.
Customer may terminate for convenience with ninety days written notice with no early termination penalties after year one.
Customer may terminate this Agreement immediately for cause including failure to meet SLAs, regulatory non-compliance, or insolvency of Supplier.
Neither party shall be liable for delays caused by circumstances beyond their reasonable control including supply chain disruptions.
Force majeure events including acts of God, pandemics, labor disputes, and government actions shall excuse performance indefinitely.
Supplier shall not be liable for any supply chain disruptions regardless of duration or impact on Customer operations.
In a force majeure event Supplier shall use reasonable efforts to resume performance within thirty days and notify Customer within 48 hours.
Force majeure events exceeding thirty days shall trigger a joint review to identify alternative solutions at no additional cost to Customer.
Force majeure events exceeding thirty days shall give Customer the right to terminate without penalty and immediately engage alternative suppliers.
All intellectual property developed under this Agreement shall remain the exclusive property of Supplier.
Work product shall not be considered work for hire and all rights remain with Supplier regardless of Customer funding.
Ownership of all process improvements and innovations developed during service delivery shall vest in Supplier.
Supplier grants Customer a non-exclusive license to use any work product developed specifically for Customer under this Agreement.
Jointly developed innovations shall be owned equally by both parties with neither party requiring consent for use.
All custom work product, process improvements, and innovations funded by Customer shall be owned exclusively by Customer upon payment.
Customer agrees that Supplier shall be the exclusive supplier of services for all Customer facilities during the term.
Customer designates Supplier as sole source provider and agrees not to engage alternative suppliers.
During the exclusivity period Customer shall direct one hundred percent of volume to Supplier.
Supplier shall be designated as preferred supplier with Customer retaining the right to dual-source for business continuity purposes.
Customer commits to a minimum annual volume with no exclusivity obligation and full rights to engage additional suppliers.
Nothing in this Agreement shall restrict Customer from engaging additional suppliers at its sole discretion at any time.
All disputes shall be resolved through binding arbitration and Customer expressly waives the right to jury trial.
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Ohio with exclusive jurisdiction in Franklin County courts.
The exclusive venue for all disputes shall be the courts of Supplier home state jurisdiction.
Disputes shall be resolved in the jurisdiction where the Customer facility involved in the dispute is located.
Parties agree to attempt mediation prior to initiating formal legal proceedings in any dispute.
Governing law and dispute resolution venue shall be mutually agreed upon with neither party having home jurisdiction advantage.
Supplier shall use best efforts to deliver orders within the requested timeframe but makes no guarantee of delivery dates.
All orders are fulfilled subject to availability and Supplier assumes no liability for stock shortages or extended lead times.
Lead times are estimates only and Supplier shall not be liable for delays regardless of impact on Customer production schedules.
Supplier commits to standard lead times of five business days for stocked items with advance notification of any delays.
Supplier guarantees delivery within agreed lead times for all catalog items with expedite options available at no premium.
Supplier guarantees delivery within agreed lead times with financial penalties for late delivery exceeding twenty-four hours on critical production items.
All products are sold as-is with no warranty expressed or implied by Supplier.
Supplier disclaims all warranties including fitness for purpose and merchantability for all MRO products supplied.
Warranty is void if products are used outside Supplier recommended parameters regardless of Customer application requirements.
Supplier provides standard manufacturer warranty on all products with defective items replaced within thirty days of notification.
All products carry a minimum twelve month warranty against defects in materials and workmanship from date of delivery.
Supplier provides a minimum twenty-four month warranty on all products with immediate replacement of defective items and no cost to Customer for return shipping.
Supplier makes no commitment to maintain local inventory and all orders are subject to manufacturer availability.
Supplier may discontinue any product at any time with no obligation to provide advance notice or alternative sourcing.
During supply constraints Customer orders may be subject to allocation at Supplier sole discretion.
Supplier commits to maintaining safety stock of Customer top fifty items with a minimum thirty day supply at all times.
Supplier shall maintain agreed inventory levels for Customer critical items and provide sixty days advance notice of any product discontinuations.
Supplier shall maintain dedicated on-site or near-site inventory for Customer critical MRO items with guaranteed availability and next day delivery commitment.
All products shall be billed at Supplier current list price at time of order with no price protection guarantee.
Supplier may change catalog pricing at any time with no advance notice requirement to Customer.
Supplier reserves the right to add fuel surcharges, handling fees, and other charges at its discretion.
Agreed pricing shall be protected for twelve months with a maximum three percent annual adjustment thereafter.
Customer shall receive agreed discount off published list price with price protection for the contract term and sixty days notice of any list price changes.
All agreed pricing shall remain fixed for the contract term with no surcharges, handling fees, or fuel charges. Any new products added shall be priced at the same discount tier as existing catalog.
